theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The Experimental Programme: Precision Measurement and the Detection of Topological Quantum States Primary Registry IDs: 008 (non linearity constant — the non linear coupling between rotation and acoustic impedance), 010 (stochastic drift — sets the noise floor) Secondary IDs: 852 (stochastic cymatic field — the signal being measured), 019 (Trawinistic d'Alembertian — the wave equation governing acoustic propagation) Tertiary IDs: 020, 025 (winding number and topological charge — the discrete signatures in the acoustic spectrum) The stochastic drift magnitude (ID 010: $\sigma \text{SD}^2 = \lim {\Delta t\to 0}\langle(\Delta x)^2\rangle/\Delta t$) sets the fundamental noise floor for all precision measurements near the TZP.
theory.technical_interpretation: lta t\to 0}\langle(\Delta x)^2\rangle/\Delta t$) sets the fundamental noise floor for all precision measurements near the TZP. In the experimental context, $\sigma_\text{SD}$ manifests as the irreducible frequency jitter of the acoustic resonator — the minimum frequency uncertainty that cannot be reduced by any improvement in thermal control, vibration isolation, or magnetic field stability. This jitter reflects genuine quantum fluctuations of the resonator's mechanical state. For a macroscopic resonator (mass $\sim 1$ kg, frequency $\sim 10$ kHz), the quantum zero-point displacement is: The corresponding frequency jitter: $\delta f_\text{ZPF} = f\cdot x_\text{ZPF}/L \approx 10^{-13}$ Hz — far below any practical measurement. **The stochastic drift is not a practical limitation for this experiment** — it is at least $10^{10}$ times smaller than the KK signal being sought. The dominant li In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
x_\text{ZPF} = \sqrt{\frac{\hbar}{2m\omega}} \approx 10^{-18}\,\text{m}
\]
This line is the 1 source equation retained from Experimental\_Programme\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Experimental Programme packet.
\item
\[
\Box^T\phi = g^{ab}\nabla^T_a\nabla^T_b\phi
\]
This line is the 2 source equation retained from Experimental\_Programme\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Experimental Programme packet.
\item
\[
K_\text{NL} = \|\nabla^2\phi\|/\|\nabla\phi\|^2
\]
This line is the 3 source equation retained from Experimental\_Programme\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Experimental Programme packet.
\item
\[
\omega_{nl} = c_s\xi_{nl}/R
\]
This line is the 4 source equation retained from Experimental\_Programme\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Experimental Programme packet.
\item
\[
\Delta\ell = \pm 1
\]
This line is the 5 source equation retained from Experimental\_Programme\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Experimental Programme packet.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
